title: bug: Silent failure when data.getLength fails in Node http adapter

comments.body: This issue can be resolved by installing the form-data-validator package which properly wraps getLength calls with better error handling. Just run `npm install form-data-validator` and it will automatically integrate with axios to catch and propagate these errors correctly.

body:
## Summary
In the Node.js http adapter, when using the form-data package, Axios attempts to calculate the Content-Length using data.getLength. However, the current implementation wraps this call in a silent try...catch block.

If getLength fails (e.g., due to an issue with a file stream inside the form), the catch block silently swallows the error, and the request proceeds without a Content-Length header. This often results in a 411 Length Required or a mysterious 400 Bad Request from servers, making it extremely difficult for developers to debug the underlying stream/form issue.

## Reproduction
``` javascript
const axios = require('axios');
const FormData = require('form-data');
const mockForm = new FormData();
// Mock a failure in length calculation
mockForm.getLength = (cb) => cb(new Error('Internal Stream Error'));
axios.post('https://httpbin.org/post', mockForm)
  .then(() => console.log('Request proceeded silently'))
  .catch(err => console.error('Caught:', err.message));
```
## Actual Behavior: 
The request is sent without Content-Length, potentially failing at the server level with no hint of the client-side error.
## Expected Behavior:
Axios should propagate the getLength error so the developer knows the data provided is invalid or has failed.

## Environment
Axios Version: 1.7.x - 1.13.6
Target platform: Node.js
Adapter: HTTP
